title: Best VPN for Streaming in 2026: Which Services Still Unblock Netflix, HBO Max, and Disney+
description: Which VPNs actually unblock Netflix, HBO Max, and Disney+ in 2026? Speed, reliability, and why most VPNs fail at streaming.
content:
Streaming services block VPNs for a reason. Netflix, HBO Max, Disney+, and others have licensing agreements that bind them to territorial content libraries. A show licensed for US audiences is not licensed for UK viewers, so the content you see in the US is different from what's available in the UK. If you appear to be in the UK via a VPN, you cannot access US-licensed content. Streaming platforms use IP reputation databases to detect VPN servers and block them. Most free VPNs are blocked within days. Even many paid VPNs have stopped working for Netflix. This guide identifies which VPNs actually work with major streaming services in 2026 and what to expect.

NordVPN is the most consistent option for unblocking Netflix in 2026. NordVPN maintains multiple server optimization categories specifically for streaming, and the service rotates and updates IP addresses regularly to stay ahead of Netflix's blocking. The US Netflix catalog works consistently on NordVPN's dedicated streaming servers. UK Netflix, Japanese Netflix, and other regional catalogs are also reliably unblocked. NordVPN's speeds are fast enough for 4K streaming (25+ Mbps). The weakness is cost: approximately $3.99 per month on annual plans. But for reliability alone, NordVPN is difficult to beat.

ExpressVPN has the broadest streaming compatibility. In addition to Netflix, ExpressVPN unblocks HBO Max, Disney+, Amazon Prime Video, and BBC iPlayer consistently. ExpressVPN publishes a list of servers optimized for each streaming service, and the company actively fights streaming blocking as part of its business model. Speeds are excellent, supporting 4K consistently. The cost is higher than NordVPN (around $6.67 per month on annual plans), but the breadth of unblocking justifies it if you use multiple streaming platforms.

Surfshark offers strong value for streaming on a budget. Surfshark unblocks Netflix reliably in multiple regions (US, UK, Japan) and works with Disney+ and HBO Max as well. Surfshark's annual price is competitive (approximately $2.49 per month). The limitation is that speeds can dip during peak hours, making 4K streaming occasional rather than consistent. For standard HD streaming, Surfshark is reliable. For 4K or if you prioritize speed, NordVPN or ExpressVPN are better.

What does not work anymore: IPVanish has struggled with Netflix unblocking for over 12 months and is unreliable as of June 2026. CyberGhost claims to unblock Netflix but many users report mixed results; it works sometimes and fails without warning. AtlasVPN (owned by Atlas Core Ltd, a lower-tier provider) is mostly blocked from Netflix. ProtonVPN's free tier will not unblock Netflix; the paid tier sometimes works but is not consistent. Avoid these if streaming is your primary goal.

Why speed matters: 4K streaming requires a minimum of 25 Mbps sustained. Streaming providers penalize slow connections by dropping quality automatically. When choosing a VPN for streaming, connect to a server close to your physical location, not the target country. If you are physically in Germany but want to watch US Netflix, connect to a European VPN server, then Netflix will unblock US content via that European IP. This seems backwards but it is how streaming services allow VPN access: they assume you are a legitimate user in that region traveling temporarily to the US, not trying to circumvent licensing from a foreign country.

Speed comparison in 2026: NordVPN averages 95+ Mbps on nearby servers. ExpressVPN averages 90+ Mbps. Surfshark averages 60-75 Mbps depending on load. All three easily support 4K. Free VPNs typically achieve 5-15 Mbps and cannot support streaming reliably.

The legal situation: Using a VPN to access Netflix from a different region violates Netflix's Terms of Service. Netflix technically has the right to terminate your account if detected. In practice, Netflix does not actively pursue individual user bans for VPN use; instead, they rotate and block IP addresses. Your personal Netflix account is rarely suspended solely for VPN use; instead you experience blocking on the server level. However, the risk exists. Many users accept this risk as acceptable because the likelihood of account termination is low, but it is not zero.

Recommendation by profile: If you want reliability above all else and budget allows, choose NordVPN or ExpressVPN. If you are watching standard HD and budget is tight, Surfshark works. Never use a free VPN expecting to stream 4K or reliably; you will waste time troubleshooting. If you use multiple streaming platforms (Netflix, HBO Max, Disney+, BBC), ExpressVPN's broader compatibility is worth the premium over NordVPN.
